title: Centaurium curvistamineum
intro: Centaurium curvistamineum, also known as the curled centaury, is a perennial plant species belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium curvistamineum include gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and gentaurinin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and dry extracts, which are often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health concerns.
taxonomy: Centaurium curvistamineum is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant is classified within the genus Centaurium, which contains approximately 15-20 species of gentians. Centaurium curvistamineum is typically found in rocky and mountainous areas. The classification of Centaurium curvistamineum is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphology.
